essential_question: Of everything from Lessons 11–19, what still has to be automatic before the Final?
objective: Review Unit 02 — polygons and circles, volume and surface area of solids, radicals, and the Pythagorean theorem — through mixed cumulative practice.

content.prompt: Unit 02 was Lessons 11–19 — round figures, three-dimensional solids, radicals, and the Pythagorean theorem. Without checking anything, which formula comes to mind first?

content.heading: Circles, solids, radicals, Pythagorean
bullets: Polygon angle sums and regular polygons; circles — radius, diameter, chord, arc, sector.
Volume of rectangular solids, cylinders, pyramids, cones, prisms, and spheres.
Surface area — nets, lateral area versus total area.
Simplifying radicals, rationalizing denominators, and the Pythagorean theorem.

content.preview: Session 3: Unit 03 — special triangles, proofs, similarity, transformations, and trig.

success_criteria.text: I can compute circumference and other circle facts
I can compute volume of a solid
I can simplify a radical and rationalize a denominator
I can apply the Pythagorean theorem to find a missing side

compass.note: Curriculum/Geometry/The Salas Compass/2026-W37 Year-End Final Review.md

questions.prompt: Find the circumference of a circle with radius 5, in terms of π.
Find the volume of a rectangular solid with length 4, width 3, and height 2.
questions.explain: Volume of a rectangular solid = length × width × height.

questions.prompt: A right triangle has legs 8 and 15. Find the hypotenuse.
Exit: which Unit 02 idea from today's mix would you want one more example of before the Final?
